ARES FTC Robot Code Documentation

Welcome to the ARES FTC robot code documentation. This codebase implements a command-based architecture for swerve drive with advanced sensor fusion and vision localization.

Architecture Overview

The robot code is organized into several key packages:

  • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.commandbase: Command-based framework with subsystems and commands for robot control
  • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.globals: Global configuration including robot constants and hardware definitions
  • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.util: Utility classes for motor control, sensor fusion, data logging, and calibration
  • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.hardware: Hardware drivers for custom devices like the SRS Hub
  • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.opmode: FTC OpModes for autonomous and teleop control
  • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.auto: Autonomous tuning and calibration routines
  • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.calibration: Sensor and vision calibration OpModes

Key Components

Swerve Drive System

The robot uses an octo-swerve drive configuration with four independently controlled modules. Each module can rotate its wheel direction (steering) and drive at variable speeds. Key classes:

  • OctoSwerveDrivetrainV2: Main drivetrain controller
  • OctoSwerveModuleV2: Individual swerve module with feedforward and PID control
  • SwerveEncoder: Swerve encoder interface with multiple implementations

Motor Control

Advanced motor control techniques ensure smooth, efficient operation:

  • SlewRateLimiter: Prevents sudden power changes that cause current spikes
  • CurrentLimiter: Monitors current draw and scales motor power to prevent tripping the 20A fuse

Sensor Fusion

Multiple sensors are combined using an Extended Kalman Filter for accurate localization:

  • Swerve Odometry: Position estimation from wheel encoders
  • GoBilda Pinpoint: Deadwheel odometry with IMU sensor fusion
  • Limelight Vision: AprilTag-based vision localization with multi-camera fusion
  • SensorFusionLocalizer: Combines all sensors using EKF

Configuration

All robot-specific constants are centralized in:

  • Constants: Physical dimensions, PIDF coefficients, feedforward constants, and tuning parameters
  • Robot: Robot singleton with hardware initialization and subsystem access

Getting Started

For New Developers

  1. Review the Constants class to understand robot configuration
  2. Study Drive to see how the drive subsystem integrates everything
  3. Examine TeleOpDrive for an example of a command implementation
  4. Check BaseTeleOp for the standard TeleOp OpMode structure

For Tuning and Calibration

The codebase includes comprehensive tuning and calibration tools:

  • AutoTuneFeedforward: Automatically tune kS and kV feedforward constants
  • AutoTuneSteering: Automatically tune steering PID
  • SteeringTuner: Manual steering PID tuning with real-time feedback
  • FeedforwardTuner: Manual feedforward tuning with velocity testing
  • LimelightCalibrationOpMode: Calibrate camera mount position and orientation

Control Theory

Feedforward Control

The swerve drive uses feedforward control to compensate for friction and maintain target speeds: 

motorPower = kS × sign(velocity) + kV × velocity
  • kS (Static Friction): Minimum power to overcome friction
  • kV (Velocity Constant): Power to maintain velocity

PID Control

PID feedback corrects errors between target and actual states: 

correction = Kp × error + Ki × ∫error dt + Kd × d(error)/dt

Current Limiting

To prevent tripping the 20A fuse, the drivetrain uses:

  • Slew Rate Limiting: Prevents rapid power changes
  • Current Monitoring: Scales down power when approaching limits
  • Soft Recovery: Gradually restores power when current drops

Telemetry and Logging

Comprehensive telemetry and data logging are available:

  • DataLogger: CSV logging to disk for post-match analysis
  • FTC Dashboard integration for real-time visualization
  • Per-module motor power breakdown (feedforward, PID, total)
  • Battery voltage monitoring
  • Loop timing and performance metrics

Hardware Requirements

  • Control Hub: FTC Robot Controller (Rev Control Hub or similar)
  • Drive Motors: 4x brushless motors with encoders
  • Steering Servos: 4x high-torque servos (e.g., Melon Super Servo)
  • Encoders: OctoQuad or analog absolute encoders for steering
  • IMU: Built-in Control Hub IMU or external IMU
  • Odometry: GoBilda Pinpoint or SRS Hub with deadwheels
  • Vision: Limelight3A camera(s) for AprilTag localization

Support

For questions or issues, please refer to:

  • OctoQuad Documentation
  • FTC SDK
  • Pedro Pathing
